# servicenow_jdbc_connection (Data Source)

`servicenow_jdbc_connection` data source can be used to retrieve information of a single JDBC connection in ServiceNow by name.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up an existing JDBC connection in ServiceNow by name.
data "servicenow_jdbc_connection" "example" {
  name = "example-jdbc-connection"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Display name of the JDBC connection configuration.

### Read-Only

- `active` (Boolean) If set to 'true', this property will enable the JDBC connection.
- `connection_alias` (String) Sys ID of the associated connection alias configuration (type=jdbc_connection).
- `connection_url` (String) JDBC URL for the connection, e.g. jdbc:mysql://host:3306/dbname.
- `credential` (String) Sys ID of the associated credential record (typically a JDBC credential alias).
- `database_name` (String) Database/schema name to connect to.
- `database_type` (String) Database type/driver, e.g. mysql, postgres, oracle, sqlserver. Maps to ServiceNow's jdbc_driver column.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `mid_selection` (String) Decides which MID server is used. auto_select or specific_mid_server.
- `mid_server` (String) Sys ID of associated MID server if a specific MID server is selected.
- `use_mid_server` (Boolean) If true, the JDBC connection will be routed through a MID server.
